title: Chewy Vietnamese Cacao Cookies
description: Herb-infused, chewy chocolate cookies that are glutenfree, sugarfree, and packed with Vietnamese flavors like lemongrass and ginger.

ingredients.name: Coconut oil, melted
ingredients.notes: use a small saucepan or microwave

ingredients.name: Flaxseed meal
ingredients.notes: combine with 3 tbsp water to form a gel

instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together oat flour, almond flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt until well blended.
In a separate bowl, whisk melted coconut oil, monk fruit sweetener, coconut milk, vanilla extract, and the prepared flaxseed gel until smooth.
Fold in minced lemongrass and chopped ginger, then pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients. Stir just until combined; the dough will be somewhat sticky.
Cover the dough and refrigerate for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to marinate.
Using a tablespoon, scoop dough onto the prepared sheet and roll into balls. Slightly flatten each ball to form cookie shapes.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, or until the edges are set but the centers remain soft.
Remove from oven and let the cookies cool on the sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
Optional: dust the cooled cookies with extra cocoa powder for an extra chocolate finish.
Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to a week.
